title: Easy, Simple Language
description: The TR doc must use easy, plain language a learner can follow on first read. Avoid needlessly formal or heavy vocabulary; explain any necessary jargon.
criterion: Plain words over ornate ones; short clauses; every technical term is explained on first use.

rubric.5: Consistently plain and clear; jargon explained.
rubric.1: Dense/ornate language or unexplained jargon that blocks understanding.

input.text: TCP checks that data arrived. If a piece is missing, it sends it again.
TCP furnishes a reliability abstraction by leveraging retransmission heuristics contingent upon acknowledgement telemetry.

cases.rationale: Ornate, heavy vocabulary — hard to follow.

input.text: Nagle's algorithm coalesces MSS-sized segments (no prior definition of MSS or Nagle given).

cases.rationale: Unexplained jargon (MSS, Nagle) on first use.
